name: Bobby Watson
abstract: Robert Michael Watson Jr. (born August 23, 1953), known professionally as Bobby Watson, is an American saxophonist, composer, and educator.

interviews.title: Bobby Watson interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, January 19, 1996

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Centennial Festival: A Celebration of Jazz: The Carnegie Hall Jazz Band
JVC Jazz Festival: Bobby McFerrin & Friends, plus The New York Giants
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Legacy of Miles
JVC Jazz Festival: A Jazzmobile Benefit
Sir Simon Rattle: Classic Ellington
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: With Wynton on trumpet, Bobby Watson on alto, Billy Pierce tenor, Charles Fambrough on bass, James Williams on piano and Bu, Art Blakey on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Bobby Watson only as part of a list of people in a group ('Billy Pierce and Bobby Watson and myself and Wynton Marsalis') introduced by the interviewer, but provides no personal interaction or relationship details in her response.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Sandke
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve had like for instance Bobby Watson, he once said to me, he said, “like I see so many white musicians being left behind, it’s not right,”
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Sandke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...I’ve heard Victor play many times live and also with Bobby Watson in a variety of settings. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Kernfeld
about_quotes.evidence: I was just there — you know turned 21, Art Blakey and the Messengers at the Keystone Korner in San Francisco with Bobby Watson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Kernfeld interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 16, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: I had a young alto player and I told him, 'You gotta hear him, Art. Bobby Watson, take him to the club.' Bobby got the job.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
